ontology.foodon_gap_note: FOODON_03301236 covers coffee beverage broadly; no specific FoodOn term exists for iced black coffee as a distinct entity.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC is the closest parent category for iced black coffee; no specific iced coffee sub-code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34731007 represents coffee as a substance; used as closest parent for iced black coffee beverage.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of iced black coffee is approximately 240 ml (one cup), served over ice with no milk or sugar added.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, coffee, brewed, prepared with tap water
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values based on USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed black coffee (NDB No. 14209 / FDC ID 171890). Iced black coffee is brewed coffee served over ice; nutrient profile per 100g is essentially identical to brewed black coffee. No milk, sugar, or additives assumed.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Beverages — Black Coffee (no sugar, no milk)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Black coffee contains negligible digestible carbohydrates and has an effectively zero glycemic index. The University of Sydney GI Database confirms that unsweetened black coffee does not raise blood glucose meaningfully. glycemic_index set to 1 (the minimum valid positive sentinel value) per Project Veritas convention for zero-GI beverages. gi_category is 'low' (GI ≤ 55). glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g per serving is effectively 0.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (2 mg per 100g); caffeine may cause transient blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Iced black coffee is very low in sodium and does not contribute meaningfully to dietary sodium load; however, individuals with hypertension should be aware that caffeine can cause a transient, short-term rise in blood pressure and should moderate total daily coffee intake to 1–2 cups.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate content; effectively zero glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: Unsweetened iced black coffee contains no digestible carbohydrates and has an effectively zero glycemic index, making it suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without added sugar or high-calorie milk; epidemiological evidence also suggests moderate coffee consumption may be associated with reduced risk of Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat and cholesterol; moderate caffeine intake
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Iced black coffee contains no saturated fat, trans fat, or cholesterol, and does not contribute to atherogenic dietary load; moderate consumption (up to 3–4 cups per day) has been associated with neutral to mildly protective cardiovascular effects in observational studies, though individuals with arrhythmias should consult their physician regarding caffeine intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Black Coffee is a chilled beverage made from brewed coffee served over ice, with no milk, sugar, or other additives. In the context of the Singapore food-delivery platform field survey, 'Iced Black' is the menu name used by To;asta (Circular Road), a Japanese pasta and udon restaurant, for their iced black coffee offering. The drink is prepared by brewing coffee — typically espresso, drip, or cold-brew style — and serving it chilled over ice. It has a bold, slightly bitter flavour with subtle roasted notes and a clean, refreshing finish due to the ice dilution. Nutritionally, iced black coffee is extremely low in calories (approximately 2 kcal per 100 ml), contains negligible fat, carbohydrate, and sugar, and has a very low sodium content. It is naturally vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. The glycemic index is effectively zero as there are no digestible carbohydrates present, making it suitable for individuals managing blood glucose levels. The primary bioactive compound is caffeine, which provides stimulant effects. Iced black coffee is widely consumed across Singapore, Japan, and globally as a morning or afternoon beverage. It is appropriate for most dietary patterns including low-carbohydrate, ketogenic, and diabetic diets when consumed without added sugar.
common_user_queries: Is iced black coffee good for diabetics?
How many calories in iced black coffee?
Does iced black coffee have sugar?
What is the GI of black coffee?
Can I drink iced black coffee if I have hypertension?
Is iced black coffee the same as cold brew?

confidence_reason: The food name 'Iced Black' from a Japanese pasta/udon restaurant (To;asta, Circular Road, Singapore) is interpreted as iced black coffee based on standard café menu conventions. No explicit product description was available in the input record. Nutrient values are sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for brewed black coffee, which is the closest approved-source match. Confidence is medium rather than high because the exact preparation method (espresso-based, drip, or cold brew) is not confirmed, and slight dilution from ice is not precisely quantified.
notes: 1. food_group corrected from 'Grains and staples' (input) to 'Beverages' as iced black coffee is a beverage, not a grain or staple. 2. fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for brewed black coffee — naturally zero as black coffee contains no fat-bearing components. 3. carbohydrate_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central — naturally zero as brewed black coffee contains no digestible carbohydrates. 4. sugar_g is 0.0: naturally zero as no sugar is added and coffee contains no free sugars. 5. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central — naturally zero for brewed liquid coffee. 6. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero as black coffee contains no cholesterol. 7. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 8. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 9. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: naturally zero as coffee is a plant-derived beverage. 10. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: naturally zero as coffee contains no vitamin D. 11. vitamin_e_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 12. selenium_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 13. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are 0.0: naturally zero as black coffee contains no fat. 14. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Project Veritas convention for zero-GI beverages. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate content per serving is effectively 0. 15. Cuisine tagged as 'Japanese' per input cuisine_tags; Japanese local name provided accordingly. 16. Input food_id GD003383 preserved. Source restaurant: To;asta - Circular Road, Singapore (field survey data).
